title: Darbhanga Fort
tagline: Darbhanga Fort — heritage city of Bihar

overview.short: Darbhanga Fort, is a monument located in the city of Darbhanga in Bihar, India.
overview.description: Darbhanga Fort, is a monument located in the city of Darbhanga in Bihar, India. It is also known as Rambagh Fort because it is situated in Rambagh Palace inside the fort. It is built by Maithali Brahman rulers. Rambagh campus is surrounded by walls and covers about 85 acres (34 ha).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Shyama Mai Temple
topPlaces.description: Mother Kali's Dham Shyama Kali temple is a Hindu temple, built on the pyre in Darbhanga, Bihar. A large number of devotees visit here and all Manglik works are also done.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic, Darbhanga
topPlaces.description: Government Polytechnic, Darbhanga is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.

topPlaces.name: Darbhanga division
topPlaces.description: Darbhanga Division is an administrative geographical unit of Bihar state of India, and Darbhanga city is the administrative headquarters of the division. It is a part of historical Mithila region.

topPlaces.name: Darbhanga
topPlaces.description: Darbhanga is the sixth largest city and municipal corporation in the Indian state of Bihar in India, and is considered an important city in North Bihar. It serves as the headquarters of the Darbhanga district and the Darbhanga division.

topPlaces.name: Harahi Lake
topPlaces.description: Harahi Lake (Hindi/Devanagari: हराही पोखर) is located near Darbhanga Railway Station, Darbhanga District, Bihar, India. There are two other lakes, Ganga Sagar Lake and Dighi Lake, which are situated in a line, with a drive passing from one to the other, and their united length is 6,000 feet.

topPlaces.name: Mitheshwarnath Shiva Temple
topPlaces.description: Mitheshwarnath Shiva Temple (Hindi/Devanagari: मीठेश्वरनाथ शिव मंदिर) is a Hindu Temple, dedicated to the Hindu god "Shiva". This temple is located at Chunabhatti, near Mitthu Mistri Chowk, Darbhanga District, Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Baba Mukteshwar Dham
topPlaces.description: Baba Mukteshwarnath Dham is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in the village of Deohar in the Indian state of Bihar. Baba Mukteshwarnath Dham is considered as one of the best temples in Deohar, Bihar.

topPlaces.name: Mohanpur, Darbhanga
topPlaces.description: Sara Mohanpur (Sara Mohammad) is a village in Darbhanga District of Bihar, located 4 km from the Darbhanga Railway Station and 9 km from the Darbhanga Airport. It is directly connected with the main city.

items.note: Mother Kali's Dham Shyama Kali temple is a Hindu temple, built on the pyre in Darbhanga, Bihar. A large number of devotees visit here and all Manglik works are also done.
Government Polytechnic, Darbhanga is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.
Darbhanga Division is an administrative geographical unit of Bihar state of India, and Darbhanga city is the administrative headquarters of the division. It is a part of historical Mithila region.

Darbhanga is the sixth largest city and municipal corporation in the Indian state of Bihar in India, and is considered an important city in North Bihar. It serves as the headquarters of the Darbhanga district and the Darbhanga division.

Harahi Lake (Hindi/Devanagari: हराही पोखर) is located near Darbhanga Railway Station, Darbhanga District, Bihar, India. There are two other lakes, Ganga Sagar Lake and Dighi Lake, which are situated in a line, with a drive passing from one to the other, and their united length is 6,000 feet.

Mitheshwarnath Shiva Temple (Hindi/Devanagari: मीठेश्वरनाथ शिव मंदिर) is a Hindu Temple, dedicated to the Hindu god "Shiva". This temple is located at Chunabhatti, near Mitthu Mistri Chowk, Darbhanga District, Bihar, India.

Baba Mukteshwarnath Dham is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in the village of Deohar in the Indian state of Bihar. Baba Mukteshwarnath Dham is considered as one of the best temples in Deohar, Bihar.

Sara Mohanpur (Sara Mohammad) is a village in Darbhanga District of Bihar, located 4 km from the Darbhanga Railway Station and 9 km from the Darbhanga Airport. It is directly connected with the main city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Darbhanga Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Darbhanga Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Darbhanga Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Muzaffarpur Junction (51 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Darbhanga Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Darbhanga Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Darbhanga Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Darbhanga Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Darbhanga Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shyama Mai Temple, Government Polytechnic, Darbhanga, Darbhanga division, Darbhanga, Harahi Lake.
